when_to_pick: Best precision, recall, and F1 among these three on this hold-out split—use as the default unless you have a different cost tradeoff than 0.5 threshold assumes.
when_to_avoid: Not a substitute for browser blocklists, TLS inspection, or human review in production.

when_to_pick: Use this model if its precision/recall mix matches your cost tradeoff; compare the chart and table.
when_to_avoid: Avoid as the sole choice when false positives are very costly—another model had higher precision.
Avoid when missing phishing is unacceptable—another model achieved higher recall.

when_to_pick: Use this model if its precision/recall mix matches your cost tradeoff; compare the chart and table.
when_to_avoid: Avoid as the sole choice when false positives are very costly—another model had higher precision.
Avoid when missing phishing is unacceptable—another model achieved higher recall.

tradeoff_summary: Phishing detection trades precision (blocking bad URLs without annoying users) against recall (catching abusive URLs). Metrics are on a single stratified hold-out split; they benchmark these pipelines on this snapshot, not production security.
